---
title: "Simulation_processing"
output: pdf_document
date: "2025-11-18"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(parallel)
library(viridis)
source(file.path(Sys.getenv("LEPTO_BURDEN_ROOT", "."), "R", "paths.R"))

#######################################Convergence################################################

file_list <- list.files(
  simulation_path("Simulation_sensitivity"),
  pattern = "*.rds",
  full.names = TRUE
)

num_cores <- detectCores()
set <- 1:length(file_list)
```

## Convergence

```{r conv}
# 2. Nested Extraction: File -> List of Models -> Rhats & Checks
results_list <- mclapply(set, function(i) {
  x <- file_list[i]
  fname <- basename(x)
  
  # Extract file-level parameters
  nh <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=nh_)\\d+"))
  sd <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=sens_spec_)\\d*\\.?\\d+"))
  job  <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=job_)\\d+"))
  
  # Read the list of lists
  sim_list <- readRDS(x)
  
  # INNER LOOP: Iterate through every simulation model
  map_dfr(sim_list, function(single_model) {
    
    # Robust check: ensure the model has rhats (skip if NULL/failed sim)
    if (is.null(single_model) || is.null(single_model$rhat)) return(NULL)
    
    rhat_vals <- single_model$rhat
    es_val <- single_model$es_2024
    
    # Check if ES is valid (not NA)
    # We use isTRUE to safely handle cases where es_val might be NULL or logical(0)
    es_is_valid <- !is.na(es_val)
    
    tibble(
      nh = nh,
      sd = sd,
      job = job,
      
      # Definition: All Rhats < 1.1 AND es_2024 is not NA
      
      converged_1_5 = all(rhat_vals < 1.5, na.rm = TRUE) & es_is_valid,
      converged_1_1 = all(rhat_vals < 1.1, na.rm = TRUE) & es_is_valid,
      
      # Definition: All Rhats < 1.01 AND es_2024 is not NA
      converged_1_01 = all(rhat_vals < 1.01, na.rm = TRUE) & es_is_valid,
      es_2024 = single_model$es_2024,
      crps = single_model$crps,
      bias_2024 = single_model$bias_2024,
      rate_cover_2024=single_model$rate_2024_cover
    )
  })
  
}, mc.cores = num_cores)

# 3. Combine and Summarize
summary_df <- results_list %>%
  bind_rows() %>%                           
  group_by(nh, sd) %>%                    
  summarise(
    total_sims = n(),
    percent_less_1_5=mean(converged_1_5, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    percent_less_1_1 = mean(converged_1_1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    percent_less_1_01 = mean(converged_1_01, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

```


## Forecasting

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r forecast, echo=FALSE}
results_list <- mclapply(set, function(i) {
  x <- file_list[i]
  fname <- basename(x)
  
  # Extract parameters from filename
  nh <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=nh_)\\d+"))
  sd <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=sens_spec_)\\d*\\.?\\d+"))
  
  # Read the list of simulations
  sim_list <- readRDS(x)
  
  # Iterate through simulations in this file
  map_dfr(sim_list, function(sim) {
    
    
    
    # 3. If passed, extract metrics
    tibble(
      nh = nh,
      sd = sd,
      es_2024 = sim$es_2024,
      crps = sim$crps,
      bias_2024 = sim$bias_2024,
      rate_cover_2024=sim$rate_2024_cover
    )
  })
  
}, mc.cores = num_cores)

# --- 2. AGGREGATION AND TRANSFORMATION ---
# (This part remains largely the same, but now calculates stats only on converged models)

summary_df <- results_list %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  # Calculate medians per group
  group_by(nh, sd) %>%
  summarise(
    median_es = median(es_2024, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_crps = median(crps, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_percent_bias = (exp(mean(bias_2024,na.rm=T))-1)*100,
    mean_coverage=mean(rate_cover_2024,na.rm=T),
    # Optional: You might want to track how many models survived the filter
    n_converged = sum(!is.na(es_2024)), 
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # Map nh to the specific X-axis values requested
  mutate(
    nh_days = case_when(
      nh == 0 ~ 4,
      nh == 1 ~ 7,
      nh == 2 ~ 14,
      nh == 3 ~ 21,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) 

# --- 3. VISUALIZATION ---

long_df <- summary_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(median_es, median_crps, mean_percent_bias,mean_coverage),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(metric = recode(metric,
                         "median_es" = "Median ES",
                         "median_crps" = "Median CRPS",
                         "mean_percent_bias" = "Mean Percent Bias",
                         "mean_coverage" = "Mean Coverage"
  ))

# Create reference lines for specific metrics
ref_lines <- tibble(
  metric = c("Mean Coverage", "Mean Percent Bias"),
  yintercept = c(95, 0)
)

# Reference lines for specific facets
ref_lines <- tibble(
  metric = c("Mean Coverage", "Mean Percent Bias"),
  yintercept = c(95, 0)
)

ggplot(long_df, aes(x = nh_days, y = value, color = factor(sd))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_hline(
    data = ref_lines,
    aes(yintercept = yintercept),
    linetype = "dotted",
    color = "red",
    linewidth = 1,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~metric, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(4, 7, 14, 21)) + 
  scale_color_viridis_d(
    name = "SD",
    labels = function(x) scales::comma(as.numeric(x))
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Number of Hospitals",
    y = "Estimate Value"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
  )

```
